acceptanceCriteria: docs/adr/0001-provenance-trust-model.md gives every proposed claim a producer and an independent verifier across release tag, source commit, dist/rigor.cjs, plugin distribution, local evidence, CI evidence, workflow identity, and Claude/Codex runtime model identity.
The ADR states which controls add security beyond current CI re-derivation and rejects checksum-beside-artifact, bundle-only attestation, and unauthenticated SLSA formats as sufficient provenance.
Keyless GitHub OIDC/Sigstore, GitHub Artifact Attestations, and SLSA-compatible options are evaluated against requirements with an adopted option and recorded rejected alternatives.
Missing consumer enforcement (no confirmed pre-activation verification point in the ordinary Claude Code marketplace flow) is documented as a blocker for #26, not hidden.
Implementation is split into independently testable follow-ups: #25 producer provenance and #26 consumer enforcement, with explicit completion and blocked conditions.

docs/threat-model.md, docs/architecture.md, docs/release.md, and docs/mvp-limitations.md reference the ADR without claiming an implemented attestation, SLSA Build Level, runtime model attestation, or consumer verifier.
CHANGELOG.md documents the ADR under an Unreleased heading following the one-entry-per-issue convention.
npm run test:all (format, lint, typecheck, tests, plugin structure validation, local link checks), npm run bench, and claude plugin validate . --strict pass on the merged tree.
note: Policy permits transmission; minimize content and re-check destination controls.
